title: Authors and Citation
text: Christopher T. Kenny. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kenny C (2026). gtscales: Color Scale Extensions 'gt'. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://christophertkenny.com/gtscales/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt'},   author = {Christopher T. Kenny},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://christophertkenny.com/gtscales/}, }

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: goal gtscales make color-encoded gt tables easier read adding matched legends directly rendered output. main interface set gtscale_data_color_*() helpers color column add matching legend one call. also reusable spec workflow cases want define scale , apply , legendize , render separately. package currently focuses three common legend types: continuous gradients numeric color scales stepped legends binned values quantile-based legends ranked values discrete swatches categorical encodings currently validated workflows : HTML rendering gt LaTeX/PDF rendering gt Quarto Typst standalone legend rendering Quarto example workflow

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscales currently provides small family helpers explaining color encodings gt tables:

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: can install development version gtscales GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("christopherkenny/gtscales")

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: examples use primary one-call wrappers. README uses saved images page stays lightweight GitHub.

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_continuous() colors column adds matching gradient legend.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_continuous(     column = num,     palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),     title = 'Numeric scale'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_bins() useful color mapping interval-based.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_bins(     column = currency,     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000, 70000),     title = 'Currency bins'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_discrete() useful colors encode compact status class variable benefits legend.
code: data.frame(   district = c('A', 'B', 'C', 'D'),   status = c('Safe D', 'Toss-up', 'Lean R', 'Safe R'),   margin = c(18, 2, -6, -21) ) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_discrete(     column = status,     values = c('#2166ac', '#f7f7f7', '#ef8a62', '#b2182b'),     labels = c('Safe D', 'Toss-up', 'Lean R', 'Safe R'),     title = 'Race rating'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_quantiles() useful want evenly sized rank groups instead fixed numeric cutpoints.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_quantiles(     column = num,     palette = c('#fdd49e', '#fdbb84', '#ef6548', '#990000'),     quantiles = 4,     title = 'Quartiles'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: want composable workflow, can build gtscale_spec first apply , render legend, together. spec workflow foundation rendering beyond default HTML case, including LaTeX Typst workflows. can also render legend directly another backend: returns Typst markup generated scale spec used HTML LaTeX paths.
code: spec <- gtscale_spec_continuous(   num,   palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),   title = 'Numeric scale' ) |>   gtscale_spec_set_application(apply_to = 'fill') |>   gtscale_spec_set_legend(output = 'html', placement = 'source_note')  exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_apply_legend(spec) spec <- gtscale_spec_quantiles(   num,   palette = c('#fdd49e', '#fdbb84', '#ef6548', '#990000'),   quantiles = 4,   title = 'Quartiles' )  gtscale_render_legend(   spec = spec,   data = gt::gt(gt::exibble),   output = 'typst' )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: repository also includes small backend-specific Quarto examples inst/examples: html-example.qmd latex-example.qmd typst-example.qmd LaTeX Typst examples also rendered PDFs folder quick inspection.

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: moment, legends attached source notes, keeps easy add existing gt pipelines. usage, gtscale_data_color_*() wrappers default choice, lower-level gtscale_color_*() functions gtscale_spec_*() constructors cases want control. Word/docx currently supported.

title: Apply a gtscales spec and attach its legend — gtscale_apply_legend
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Apply a gtscales spec and attach its legend — gtscale_apply_legend
code: library(gt)  spec <- gtscale_spec_continuous(   num,   palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),   title = 'Value' )  exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_apply_legend(spec)     num       char       fctr       date       time       datetime       currency       row       group     1.111e-01 apricot one 2015-01-15 13:35 2018-01-01 02:22 49.950 row_1 grp_a2.222e+00 banana two 2015-02-15 14:40 2018-02-02 14:33 17.950 row_2 grp_a3.333e+01 coconut three 2015-03-15 15:45 2018-03-03 03:44 1.390 row_3 grp_a4.444e+02 durian four 2015-04-15 16:50 2018-04-04 15:55 65100.000 row_4 grp_a5.550e+03 NA five 2015-05-15 17:55 2018-05-05 04:00 1325.810 row_5 grp_bNA fig six 2015-06-15 NA 2018-06-06 16:11 13.255 row_6 grp_b7.770e+05 grapefruit seven NA 19:10 2018-07-07 05:22 NA row_7 grp_b8.880e+06 honeydew eight 2015-08-15 20:20 NA 0.440 row_8 grp_bValue08,880,000

what: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
text: lower-level helper cases table coloring already handled elsewhere. usual "color legendize" workflow, prefer gtscale_data_color_bins().

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
code: gtscale_color_bins(   data,   column = NULL,   palette,   domain = NULL,   bins,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   width = "180px",   height = "14px" )

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column optional numeric column underlying table used infer domain. palette vector colors palette endpoints used color bins. domain numeric vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. bins numeric vector bin boundaries. labels labeling function character vector bins. function supplied, applied bin boundaries interval labels constructed. title Optional legend title. width Width legend. height Height swatches.

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
code: library(gt)  exibble |>   gt() |>   data_color(     columns = currency,     method = 'bin',     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000000)   ) |>   gtscale_color_bins(     column = currency,     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000000),     title = 'Binned values'   ) #> Warning: Some values were outside the color scale and will be treated as NA     num       char       fctr       date       time       datetime       currency       row       group     1.111e-01 apricot one 2015-01-15 13:35 2018-01-01 02:22 49.950 row_1 grp_a2.222e+00 banana two 2015-02-15 14:40 2018-02-02 14:33 17.950 row_2 grp_a3.333e+01 coconut three 2015-03-15 15:45 2018-03-03 03:44 1.390 row_3 grp_a4.444e+02 durian four 2015-04-15 16:50 2018-04-04 15:55 65100.000 row_4 grp_a5.550e+03 NA five 2015-05-15 17:55 2018-05-05 04:00 1325.810 row_5 grp_bNA fig six 2015-06-15 NA 2018-06-06 16:11 13.255 row_6 grp_b7.770e+05 grapefruit seven NA 19:10 2018-07-07 05:22 NA row_7 grp_b8.880e+06 honeydew eight 2015-08-15 20:20 NA 0.440 row_8 grp_bBinned values0 - 1010 - 100100 - 1,0001,000 - 10,000,000

what: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous

text: lower-level helper cases table coloring already handled elsewhere. usual "color legendize" workflow, prefer gtscale_data_color_continuous().

title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
code: gtscale_color_continuous(   data,   column = NULL,   palette = NULL,   domain = NULL,   breaks = NULL,   labels = scales::label_comma(),   title = NULL,   direction = "to right",   width = "160px",   height = "14px",   fn = NULL )

title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column optional numeric column underlying table used infer domain. palette vector colors used legend gradient. domain numeric vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. breaks Optional numeric break values display gradient. labels labeling function character vector breaks. title Optional legend title. direction CSS gradient direction. Defaults "right". width Width legend bar. height Height legend bar. fn Backward-compatible fallback passing scales palette function. palette preferred.

title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete

text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete
code: library(gt)  data.frame(   category = c('Low', 'Medium', 'High'),   value = c(12, 47, 83) ) |>   gt() |>   data_color(     columns = category,     method = 'factor',     palette = c('#1b9e77', '#d95f02', '#7570b3')   ) |>   gtscale_color_discrete(     values = c('#1b9e77', '#d95f02', '#7570b3'),     labels = c('Low', 'Medium', 'High'),     title = 'Category'   )     category       value     Low 12Medium 47High 83CategoryLowMediumHigh

title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins
text: primary interface binned scales gtscales.

title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins
code: gtscale_data_color_bins(   data,   column,   palette,   bins,   domain = NULL,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   width = "180px",   height = "14px",   apply_to = c("fill", "text"),   na_color = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   reverse = FALSE,   autocolor_text = TRUE,   contrast_algo = c("apca", "wcag"),   autocolor_light = "#FFFFFF",   autocolor_dark = "#000000" )

title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column numeric column color legendize. palette vector colors palette endpoints used bins. bins numeric vector bin boundaries. domain numeric vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. labels labeling function character vector bins. function supplied, applied bin boundaries interval labels constructed. title Optional legend title. width Width legend. height Height swatches. apply_to Whether colors applied cell fill text. na_color Color used missing values. alpha Alpha applied gt::data_color(). reverse Whether reverse color mapping. autocolor_text Whether automatically adjust text color. contrast_algo Contrast algorithm passed gt::data_color(). autocolor_light Light text color used gt::data_color(). autocolor_dark Dark text color used gt::data_color().

text: Attach legend component gtscales spec

title: Attach only the legend component of a gtscales spec — gtscale_legend

title: Attach only the legend component of a gtscales spec — gtscale_legend
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). spec gtscale_spec.

title: Attach only the legend component of a gtscales spec — gtscale_legend
text: modified gt::gt table.

what: Render a legend from a gtscale_spec — gtscale_render_legend
title: Render a legend from a gtscale_spec — gtscale_render_legend
text: Render legend gtscale_spec

title: Render a legend from a gtscale_spec — gtscale_render_legend
code: gtscale_render_legend(   spec,   data = NULL,   output = c("contextual", "html", "latex", "typst") )

title: Render a legend from a gtscale_spec — gtscale_render_legend
text: spec gtscale_spec. data optional gt_tbl used finalize specs infer domains quantile boundaries table data. output Output target. Use "html", "latex", "typst", "contextual".

title: Render a legend from a gtscale_spec — gtscale_render_legend
text: Rendered legend content requested output target.

title: Render a legend from a gtscale_spec — gtscale_render_legend
code: spec <- gtscale_spec_quantiles(   num,   palette = c('#fdd49e', '#fdbb84', '#ef6548', '#990000'),   quantiles = 4,   title = 'Quartiles' )  gtscale_render_legend(   spec = spec,   data = gt::gt(gt::exibble),   output = 'latex' ) #> [1] "\\textbf{Quartiles}\\\\\\textcolor[HTML]{FDD49E}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 0 - 18\\quad \\textcolor[HTML]{FDBB84}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 18 - 444\\quad \\textcolor[HTML]{EF6548}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 444 - 391,275\\quad \\textcolor[HTML]{990000}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 391,275 - 8,880,000"

what: Create a binned gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_bins

text: Create binned gtscales spec

title: Create a binned gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_bins
code: gtscale_spec_bins(   column,   palette,   bins,   domain = NULL,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   width = "180px",   height = "14px" )

title: Create a binned gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_bins
text: column column target. palette vector colors palette endpoints used bins. bins numeric vector bin boundaries. domain Optional numeric limits. omitted, can inferred spec applied gt table. labels labeling function character vector legend. title Optional legend title. width Width legend. height Height swatches.

title: Create a binned gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_bins
text: gtscale_spec.

text: Create continuous gtscales spec

title: Create a continuous gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_continuous
code: gtscale_spec_continuous(   column,   palette = NULL,   domain = NULL,   breaks = NULL,   labels = scales::label_comma(),   title = NULL,   direction = "to right",   width = "160px",   height = "14px",   fn = NULL )

title: Create a continuous gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_continuous
text: column column target. palette vector colors used scale. domain Optional numeric limits. omitted, can inferred spec applied gt table. breaks Optional numeric break values legend. labels labeling function character vector legend. title Optional legend title. direction CSS gradient direction. Defaults "right". width Width legend bar. height Height legend bar. fn Optional scales function numeric coloring.

title: Create a continuous gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_continuous
text: gtscale_spec.

title: Create a discrete gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_discrete
text: column column target. values vector color values. labels Labels legend swatch. Defaults values. title Optional legend title. swatch_size Size discrete color swatch. levels Optional factor levels. ordered Whether scale treated ordered.

title: Create a discrete gtscales spec — gtscale_spec_discrete

what: Set how a gtscales spec is applied — gtscale_spec_set_application
title: Set how a gtscales spec is applied — gtscale_spec_set_application
text: Set gtscales spec applied

title: Set how a gtscales spec is applied — gtscale_spec_set_application
code: gtscale_spec_set_application(   spec,   apply_to = c("fill", "text"),   na_color = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   reverse = FALSE,   autocolor_text = TRUE,   contrast_algo = c("apca", "wcag"),   autocolor_light = "#FFFFFF",   autocolor_dark = "#000000" )

title: Set how a gtscales spec is applied — gtscale_spec_set_application
text: spec gtscale_spec. apply_to Whether colors applied cell fill text. na_color Color used missing values. alpha Alpha applied gt::data_color(). reverse Whether reverse color mapping. autocolor_text Whether automatically adjust text color. contrast_algo Contrast algorithm passed gt::data_color(). autocolor_light Light text color used gt::data_color(). autocolor_dark Dark text color used gt::data_color().

title: Set how a gtscales spec is applied — gtscale_spec_set_application
text: modified gtscale_spec.

what: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
title: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
text: Set gtscales legend rendered

title: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
code: gtscale_spec_set_legend(spec, output = "html", placement = "source_note")

title: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
text: spec gtscale_spec. output Output target legend. Use "contextual" gt-managed HTML/LaTeX source notes, choose specific output like "html", "latex", "typst". placement Legend placement target. Currently "source_note" implemented.

title: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
text: modified gtscale_spec.

what: gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt' — gtscales-package
title: gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt' — gtscales-package
text: Adds legend helpers color-encoded 'gt' tables. primary interface combines 'gt::data_color()' matched legends continuous, binned, quantile, discrete scales, lower-level helpers support legend-reusable scale specification workflows.

title: gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt' — gtscales-package
text: Maintainer: Christopher T. Kenny ctkenny@proton.(ORCID)
